title: BaytsAlertsRecipe
description:
A SEPAL BayTS (Bayesian Time Series) Alerts recipe. Generates per-pixel deforestation/disturbance alerts from Sentinel-1 SAR data, comparing recent observations against a `BAYTS_HISTORICAL` reference distribution. Outputs alert pixels with confidence scores.

**Pipeline.** (1) Load `reference` (a BayTS Historical recipe or its exported asset). (2) Pull recent S1 scenes covering the monitoring window ending at `date.monitoringEnd`, going back `date.monitoringDuration` months/years. (3) Compute Bayesian posterior for change at each pixel; emit alerts when probability exceeds thresholds.

reference.title: BaytsAlertsReference
reference.description: BayTS Historical reference. Either a SEPAL BayTS Historical recipe or an Earth Engine asset id of an exported one. The user must run BayTS Historical first.

date.title: BaytsAlertsDate
date.description: Monitoring window: from `monitoringEnd - monitoringDuration` to `monitoringEnd` (exclusive end).

monitoringEnd.description: End of monitoring window (exclusive). Defaults to today when absent.

monitoringDurationUnit.description: Unit for monitoringDuration. (Backend accepts `years` too, but the GUI does not expose it.)

options.title: BaytsAlertsOptions
options.description: Sentinel-1 processing options. Same shape as BAYTS_HISTORICAL.options — typically copied from the historical reference.

wetlandMaskAsset.description: Optional EE asset id for a wetland mask. Default: `users/wiell/SepalResources/wetlandMask_v1`. Set to empty string to disable.

normalization.description: Whether to normalize per-orbit means to the historical reference.

maxDays.description: Maximum days to wait for a confirmation observation. Default `90`.

highConfidenceThreshold.description: Posterior threshold for HIGH-confidence alerts. Default `0.975`.

lowConfidenceThreshold.description: Posterior threshold for LOW-confidence alerts. Default `0.85`.

minNonForestProbability.description: Minimum non-forest posterior to consider a pixel disturbed. Default `0.6`.
